ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general laksa entry (FOODON_03311737); no specific dry laksa with grilled black tiger prawn variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers Asian noodle soups as the closest parent category; no dry laksa-specific code exists in FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for dry laksa with grilled prawns. SNOMED CT does not have granular prepared dish codes at this level.

serving.description: 1 plate (approximately 400 g including noodles, sauce, and 2 grilled jumbo black tiger prawns)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 400 g, comprising a portion of rice vermicelli noodles coated in dry coconut-rempah sauce with cherry tomatoes, fresh chili, and 2 grilled jumbo black tiger prawns.

nutrient_source.title: Laksa (noodle dish) nutrient composition — adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central component data for coconut cream, rice vermicelli, and black tiger prawns
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo provides nutrient data for standard Singapore laksa (wet style). Dry laksa values are adapted by reducing water content and adjusting fat upward to reflect concentrated coconut cream sauce. Black tiger prawn contribution estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked shrimp (NDB 15152). Weighted composite per 100 g calculated from estimated dish composition: ~45% noodles, ~30% sauce (coconut cream rempah), ~20% prawns, ~5% vegetables/tomatoes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — rice vermicelli and coconut-based noodle dish analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for dry laksa with grilled black tiger prawns. GI estimated at 53 based on the closest analogous food: rice vermicelli noodles (GI ~53, University of Sydney GI Database) as the primary glycemic carbohydrate source. Coconut cream and protein from prawns are expected to attenuate glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (53 × 62.4 g available carbohydrate per 400 g serving) / 100 = 33.1, rounded to 33.2. This is an analogous estimate; direct testing is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1920 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load (~1920 mg per serving), which represents approximately 83% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; requesting reduced-salt preparation or consuming a smaller portion is strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from rice vermicelli noodles with partial mitigation from coconut fat and prawn protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that this dish carries a moderate-to-high glycemic load (~33 per serving) driven by rice vermicelli; portion control is essential, and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables may help blunt postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat from coconut cream (~5.4 g per 100 g) and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish sparingly due to its high saturated fat content from coconut cream and its high sodium level, both of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes; grilled prawns contribute dietary cholesterol (~72 mg per 100 g) and should be factored into daily limits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dry Laksa with Grilled Jumbo Black Tiger Prawns is a Singaporean noodle dish that presents a contemporary, restaurant-style interpretation of the classic laksa. Unlike the more common wet laksa served in a rich coconut-curry broth, the dry variant features rice vermicelli noodles tossed in a concentrated, intensely flavoured rempah-coconut cream sauce that clings to the noodles rather than pooling as a soup. The rempah — a traditional Peranakan spice paste — is made from lemongrass, galangal, dried shrimp, shrimp paste (belacan), candlenuts, and fresh or dried chilies, which are blended and fried until fragrant before being combined with coconut cream to create the sauce. The dish is garnished with cherry tomatoes and fresh chili, adding brightness and heat. The centrepiece protein is two grilled jumbo black tiger prawns (Penaeus monodon), which are prized for their firm, sweet flesh and characteristic striped shell. Grilling imparts a smoky char that contrasts with the rich, creamy noodle base. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense (~162 kcal per 100 g), with moderate protein (~9.2 g per 100 g) from the prawns, significant saturated fat (~5.4 g per 100 g) from coconut cream, and a moderate carbohydrate load (~15.6 g per 100 g) from rice vermicelli. Sodium is high (~480 mg per 100 g), making this dish a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low), but the glycemic load per full serving is substantial (~33), requiring portion awareness for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is halal-suitable and dairy-free. It is served at Mingle - Funan in Singapore and is priced at SGD 33.00.

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient match exists in HPB Foodo or any approved primary source for this specific dish (dry laksa with grilled jumbo black tiger prawns). Values are adapted from HPB Singapore laksa data and USDA FoodData Central component data for rice vermicelli, coconut cream, and cooked shrimp. GI is an analogous estimate based on rice vermicelli GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Cuisine corrected from 'Italian' (input error) to 'Singaporean' — the dish is a Singaporean Peranakan noodle dish; cuisine_tags 'Italian', 'Pizza', 'Pasta' from the seed record are incorrect and have been discarded. 2. Nutrient values are composite estimates; direct laboratory analysis of this specific dish from Mingle - Funan is recommended for high-confidence classification. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this dish composite. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this dish composite. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this dish composite. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as coconut cream and grilled prawns do not contribute industrial trans fats; naturally zero for this dish composition. 7. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 53) based on rice vermicelli as the primary glycemic carbohydrate; direct GI testing of this dish has not been performed. 8. Serving size estimated at 400 g based on typical Singapore restaurant noodle dish portion with 2 large prawns.
